# services.openldap

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.openldap`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.openldap.configDir` | `null or absolute path` | Use this config directory instead of generating one from the `settings` option. Overrides all NixOS settings. |
| `services.openldap.declarativeContents` | `attribute set of strings concatenated with "\n"` | Declarative contents for the LDAP database, in LDIF format by suffix. All data will be erased when starting the LDAP server. Modifications to the database are not prevented, they are just dropped on the next reboot of the server. Performance-wise the database and indexes are rebuilt on each server startup, so this will slow down server startup, especially with large databases. Note that the root of the DB must be defined in `services.openldap.settings` and the `olcDbDirectory` must begin with `"/var/lib/openldap"`. |
| `services.openldap.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the ldap server. |
| `services.openldap.group` | `string` | Group account under which slapd runs. |
| `services.openldap.mutableConfig` | `boolean` | Whether to allow writable on-line configuration. If `true`, the NixOS settings will only be used to initialize the OpenLDAP configuration if it does not exist, and are subsequently ignored. |
| `services.openldap.package` | `package` | The openldap package to use. This can be used to, for example, set an OpenLDAP package with custom overrides to enable modules or other functionality. |
| `services.openldap.settings` | `submodule` | Configuration for OpenLDAP, in OLC format |
| `services.openldap.settings.attrs` | `attribute set of ((LDAP value - either a string, or an attrset containing `path`or`base64`for included values or base-64 encoded values respectively. ) or list of (LDAP value - either a string, or an attrset containing`path`or`base64` for included values or base-64 encoded values respectively. ))` | Attributes of the parent entry. |
| `services.openldap.settings.children` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Child entries of the current entry, with recursively the same structure. |
| `services.openldap.settings.includes` | `list of absolute path` | LDIF files to include after the parent's attributes but before its children. |
| `services.openldap.urlList` | `list of string` | URL list slapd should listen on. |
| `services.openldap.user` | `string` | User account under which slapd runs. |
